summary_en: 24,000 shares (100% stake) of Ogawa Shinkoku Shoten (steel materials wholesaler, Sendai) to be acquired. Acquisition price not disclosed; third-party due diligence and valuation performed. Board decision 28 April 2026, contract executed 30 April 2026, settlement scheduled 1 July 2026. Objective: establish full Tohoku region steel-sales channel by integrating target with existing northeast regional network.
title_en: Notice Regarding Full Acquisition of All Shares of Ogawa Shinkoku Shoten Co., Ltd. (Complete Subsidiary)
translation_en_brief: Canox Corporation announced on 15 May 2026 that its board resolved on 28 April 2026 to acquire all outstanding shares (24,000 shares) of Ogawa Shinkoku Shoten Co., Ltd., a steel-materials wholesaler based in Sendai, Miyagi Prefecture, thereby making it a wholly-owned subsidiary. The company did not disclose the acquisition price, citing the counterparty's preference for confidentiality, but stated that the price was determined through third-party due diligence and valuation and is deemed fair and reasonable. The share purchase agreement was executed on 30 April 2026, with settlement scheduled for 1 July 2026. The acquisition is intended to consolidate Canox's steel-sales channels across the entire Tohoku region by integrating the target with its existing northeast sales network, thereby strengthening the group's steel supply-chain capabilities. The target company has net assets of ¥400 million and total assets of ¥616 million as of the filing date.
